response:
The inequality required is 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 ≥ 10 which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 ≥ 0 and upon testing values between 3 and 6 we find that at x=3 the value is 2(27)-5(9)+3(3)-17 = 54-45+9-17 = 1 which is less than 10 and at x=4 the value is 2(64)-5(16)+3(4)-17 = 128-80+12-17 = 43 which is greater than 10 so the range of x-values satisfying the condition within the interval is approximately x ≥ 3.24 inches up to 6 inches since the function is strictly increasing in this region.
******
To find the screen width where the area is 15 square inches we solve 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15 which becomes 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0 and by testing integer roots we find that x=3 gives 2(27)-5(9)+3(3)-22 = 54-45+9-22 = -4 and x=4 gives 2(64)-5(16)+3(4)-22 = 128-80+12-22 = 38 so the real solution lies between 3 and 4 and using numerical approximation methods we determine the width is approximately 3.33 inches while the other two roots are complex numbers which are not physically meaningful for screen width.
